name: Ernie Henry
abstract: Ernie Henry (born Ernest Albert Henry, September 3, 1926 – December 29, 1957) was an American jazz saxophonist. Henry played in the late 1940s with Tadd Dameron, Fats Navarro, Charlie Ventura, Max Roach, and was a key member of Dizzy Gillespie's big band.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I had Max on drums and Tommy Potter on bass and Ernie Henry on alto. And George on piano. And Hamp was at Birdland next door... Hamp came in and he called me over and he said, “hey, want to go to Europe?”
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: Cecil Payne [phonetic sp.], Ernie Henry [phonetic sp.]. Yeah. So. And I'm -- I was 21 years old.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Moody
about_quotes.evidence: When I joined Dizzy’s band in ‘46... Ernie Henry and then later John Brown... I was there
about_quotes.doc_title: James Moody interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Melba Liston
about_quotes.evidence: You did a record with Ernie Henry, right? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
about_quotes.doc_title: Melba Liston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Orrin Keepnews
about_quotes.evidence: Ernie Henry had been on the earlier sessions of this record, and Ernie Henry was the horn in Monk’s quartet... Monk made the statement, 'Out of my band, off my record date.' So, instead of having Ernie Henry on this final session it was Clark Terry.
about_quotes.doc_title: Orrin Keepnews — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: So, I made the other side with him, and that was Grauer and Orrin Keepnews my introduction to Bill
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
